title: Coroutine should include co_return on each execution path or provide return_void
description: Coroutine should have co_return on each execution path or provide return_voidAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugconfusingsince-c++20suspiciousunpredictableWhy is this an issue?More InfoWhen a regular, non-voidfunction flows off the end of its body without returning a value, the behavior is undefined. With a coroutine, when flowing off the end of its body,return_void()is invoked on the promise for the said coroutine. If such invocation is not possible (e.g., because the function is not defined), the behavior is undefined.In other words, a coroutine should either:have all its execution paths reach aco_returnstatement or throw an exception;or its promise type should providereturn_void().This rule raises an issue on coroutines that do not meet the above criteria.Noncompliant
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Coroutine should include co_return on each execution path or provide return_void
